verses.text: Against Ammon: why has their idol-king seized Gad's inheritance? An alarm of war shall sound in Rabbah. Theft of God's land is judged. He defends the rights He once gave His people.
Against Ammon: why has their idol-king seized Gad's inheritance? An alarm of war shall sound in Rabbah. Theft of God's land is judged. He defends the rights He once gave His people.
Howl, O Heshbon; their king and priests shall go into captivity; yet 'afterward I will bring again the captivity of... Ammon.' Judgment, then mercy. God's grace can reach even the heathen in the end.
Howl, O Heshbon; their king and priests shall go into captivity; yet 'afterward I will bring again the captivity of... Ammon.' Judgment, then mercy. God's grace can reach even the heathen in the end.
Howl, O Heshbon; their king and priests shall go into captivity; yet 'afterward I will bring again the captivity of... Ammon.' Judgment, then mercy. God's grace can reach even the heathen in the end.
Howl, O Heshbon; their king and priests shall go into captivity; yet 'afterward I will bring again the captivity of... Ammon.' Judgment, then mercy. God's grace can reach even the heathen in the end.

Against Edom: is wisdom vanished from Teman? God lays Esau bare, but says, 'leave thy fatherless children, I will preserve them.' Even in wrath, tenderness. The God who judges still cares for the helpless.

Against Edom: is wisdom vanished from Teman? God lays Esau bare, but says, 'leave thy fatherless children, I will preserve them.' Even in wrath, tenderness. The God who judges still cares for the helpless.

Against Edom: is wisdom vanished from Teman? God lays Esau bare, but says, 'leave thy fatherless children, I will preserve them.' Even in wrath, tenderness. The God who judges still cares for the helpless.

If those not sentenced must drink the cup, how shall Edom escape? Bozrah shall become a desolation and a curse. None deserves to be spared. If the comparatively innocent suffer, the guilty surely will.

If those not sentenced must drink the cup, how shall Edom escape? Bozrah shall become a desolation and a curse. None deserves to be spared. If the comparatively innocent suffer, the guilty surely will.
Edom's terribleness deceived her; though she nest like the eagle among the rocks, God will bring her down. Pride blinds. The loftiest stronghold is no defence against the LORD.
Edom's terribleness deceived her; though she nest like the eagle among the rocks, God will bring her down. Pride blinds. The loftiest stronghold is no defence against the LORD.
Edom's terribleness deceived her; though she nest like the eagle among the rocks, God will bring her down. Pride blinds. The loftiest stronghold is no defence against the LORD.
Edom shall become a desolation like Sodom, where no man dwells. Total ruin. Sin pursued to the end leaves nothing standing.
Edom shall become a desolation like Sodom, where no man dwells. Total ruin. Sin pursued to the end leaves nothing standing.
God comes up like a lion against her, asking, 'who is like me? and who will appoint me the time?' His sovereignty is absolute. None can summon Him to account or resist His purpose.
God comes up like a lion against her, asking, 'who is like me? and who will appoint me the time?' His sovereignty is absolute. None can summon Him to account or resist His purpose.

Against Damascus: Hamath and Arpad are confounded, the city feeble with fear; God will kindle a fire in her walls. The famed city falls. No reputation shields a people from God's hand.
Against Damascus: Hamath and Arpad are confounded, the city feeble with fear; God will kindle a fire in her walls. The famed city falls. No reputation shields a people from God's hand.

Against Kedar and Hazor, smitten by Nebuchadnezzar; their tents, flocks, and curtains are taken. The wandering tribes are not too remote. God's reach extends to the desert as to the city.
Against Kedar and Hazor, smitten by Nebuchadnezzar; their tents, flocks, and curtains are taken. The wandering tribes are not too remote. God's reach extends to the desert as to the city.
Flee far off, O Hazor, for Babylon has plotted against you; you shall be a haunt of jackals. The wealthy and secure are scattered. None is so settled that God cannot uproot him.
Flee far off, O Hazor, for Babylon has plotted against you; you shall be a haunt of jackals. The wealthy and secure are scattered. None is so settled that God cannot uproot him.

Against Elam: God will break the bow of Elam, their chief might, and scatter them to the four winds. Their strength is snapped. He removes the very thing a nation trusts.
Against Elam: God will break the bow of Elam, their chief might, and scatter them to the four winds. Their strength is snapped. He removes the very thing a nation trusts.
Against Elam: God will break the bow of Elam, their chief might, and scatter them to the four winds. Their strength is snapped. He removes the very thing a nation trusts.

He will set His throne in Elam and consume their rulers; 'yet... in the latter days... I will bring again the captivity of Elam.' Judgment is not the last word. God reserves mercy even for distant nations.
